title: Math — Linear Functions
description: SAT-style linear function questions focusing on slope, intercepts, rate of change, and real-world modeling.

questions.prompt: For the function \(f(x)=2x+3\), the graph of \(y=f(x)\) in the xy-plane is parallel to line \(j\). What is the slope of line \(j\)?
questions.explanation: The function \(y=2x+3\) has slope 2. Parallel lines have equal slopes, so line \(j\) also has slope 2.

questions.prompt: One gallon of paint covers 220 square feet. A room has total wall area \(w\) square feet. Which equation represents the total amount of paint \(P\), in gallons, needed to paint the walls twice?
questions.explanation: Painting twice covers \(2w\) square feet. Since 1 gallon covers 220 ft², \(P=\frac{2w}{220}=\frac{w}{110}\).

questions.prompt: The function \(f(x)\) is defined as 19 more than 4 times a number \(x\). If \(y=f(x)\) is graphed, what is the best interpretation of the x-intercept?
choices: The value of \(f(x)\) increases by 1 for each increase of 4 in the number
For each increase of 1 in the number, \(f(x)\) increases by 4
questions.explanation: The function is \(f(x)=4x+19\). The x-intercept occurs when \(f(x)=0\): \(0=4x+19\Rightarrow x=-\frac{19}{4}\).

questions.prompt: The population of Greenleaf, Idaho, was 862 in 2000 and 846 in 2010. If the relationship is linear, which function \(P\) models the population \(t\) years after 2000?
Brian saves \(\frac{2}{5}\) of the \($215\) he earns each week. How much money will Brian save in 9 weeks?
For the linear function \(h(x)=x+b\), \(h(0)=45\). What is the value of \(b\)?

For groups of 25 or more people, a museum charges \($21\) per person for the first 25 people and \($14\) for each additional person. Which function \(f\) gives the total charge for \(n\) people, where \(n\ge25\)?
The boiling point of water at sea level is 212°F. For every 550 feet above sea level, the boiling point decreases by about 1°F. Which equation gives the boiling point \(B\) at altitude \(x\) feet?
questions.explanation: The rate of change is \(-\frac{1}{550}\) °F per foot, starting at 212°F: \(B=212-\frac{x}{550}\).

questions.prompt: \(w(t)=300-4t\) models the volume of liquid (in milliliters) in a container \(t\) seconds after it begins draining. What is the predicted volume draining each second?
questions.explanation: The slope is −4, meaning the volume decreases by 4 mL each second.

questions.prompt: The equation \(y=18-5x\) represents the speed (ft/s) of Sheila’s bicycle \(x\) seconds after she applied the brakes. What does the x-intercept represent?
choices: The rate the speed decreases each second
The number of seconds until the bicycle stops
The total distance traveled while braking
questions.explanation: At the x-intercept, \(y=0\), meaning the speed is zero. The x-value is the time until the bicycle comes to a complete stop.
